prerequisites: API server running on port 5100 with MinIO (S3) available on port 9000
At least one company and user seeded (STAFF and MANAGER roles)
At least one CustomerInvoice record seeded for entityType/entityId test targets
Cross-cutting panels wired to a record detail page (e.g., /ar/invoices/$id)

journeys.name: Upload Attachment via Drag-and-Drop
journeys.description: Test the full attachment upload flow: open panel via ActionBar, drag a PDF onto the upload zone, verify presign→S3→confirm orchestration completes, and the file appears in the attachment list with correct metadata.

steps.expected_result: Invoice detail page loads with ActionBar visible
steps.visual_check: Record detail page rendered with ActionBar showing Attachments button (paperclip icon) and Links button in the persistent tools zone

steps.expected_result: Attachments button visible in persistent tools zone with paperclip icon, no count badge (0 attachments)

steps.target: Attachments button in ActionBar persistent tools zone
steps.expected_result: AttachmentPanel Sheet slides in from the right side
Sheet panel open with title 'Attachments', drag-drop upload zone visible with 'Click or drag files here' label, empty attachment list below
steps.visual_check: Right-side Sheet panel (400px width) showing: header with 'Attachments' title, dashed-border FileUploadZone with upload icon and instruction text, empty state message below

steps.target: FileUploadZone drop area (or hidden file input trigger)
steps.expected_result: File picker opens and file is selected, upload process begins automatically
Progress bar appears showing file name 'test-document.pdf', status transitions: presigning → uploading (with percentage) → confirming → complete
steps.visual_check: UploadProgressBar visible with file name, animated progress bar filling, status text showing current phase of the presign→upload→confirm flow

steps.expected_result: Attachment list refreshes and shows the uploaded file with: PDF file icon, name 'test-document.pdf', size '240 KB', relative date 'just now', download button, delete button (if MANAGER)
Badge shows '1' next to the Attachments title
steps.visual_check: AttachmentPanel showing: header with count badge '1', FileUploadZone at top, below it the uploaded file row with PDF icon, filename, size '240 KB', timestamp, and action buttons (download/delete)

steps.expected_result: AttachmentPanel closes
ActionBar Attachments button now shows count badge '1'
steps.visual_check: ActionBar persistent tools zone showing Attachments button with purple badge '(1)' next to the paperclip icon

journeys.name: Download an Attachment
journeys.description: Test that clicking the download button on an attachment generates a presigned GET URL and opens it for the user to download the file.

steps.expected_result: Invoice detail page loads
AttachmentPanel opens showing previously uploaded file

steps.target: Download button (Download icon) on first attachment row
steps.expected_result: Download button (lucide Download icon) visible on the attachment row
Browser initiates download via presigned S3 GET URL — a new tab opens or download begins (API call: GET /attachments/{id}/download returns presigned URL)
API call returned 200 with downloadUrl, fileName, and mimeType in the response

journeys.name: Reject Executable File Upload
journeys.description: Test that attempting to upload an executable file (.exe, .bat, .sh) is rejected with a validation error before the upload begins — client-side MIME/extension check in FileUploadZone.

steps.expected_result: Invoice detail page loads
AttachmentPanel opens
File is rejected before upload begins — no presign API call made
Error message displayed: file type not allowed (translation key: crossCutting.attachments.fileTypeNotAllowed)
steps.visual_check: FileUploadZone showing red validation error text below the drop area indicating that .exe files are not permitted

steps.expected_result: No new attachment added — list remains unchanged

journeys.name: Reject Oversized File Upload
journeys.description: Test that a file exceeding the 50MB size limit is rejected with a validation error before the presign request is made.

steps.expected_result: Invoice detail page loads
AttachmentPanel opens
File rejected — exceeds 50MB maximum
Error message: file too large (translation key: crossCutting.attachments.fileTooLarge)
steps.visual_check: FileUploadZone displaying red error text indicating file exceeds the maximum allowed size of 50 MB

journeys.name: Delete Attachment as MANAGER
journeys.description: Test that a MANAGER-role user can delete an attachment, the S3 object is removed, and the list updates. Also verify STAFF users cannot see the delete button.

steps.expected_result: Invoice detail page loads (logged in as MANAGER)
AttachmentPanel opens with existing attachment visible

steps.target: Delete button (Trash2 icon) on attachment row
steps.expected_result: Delete button (red trash icon) visible for MANAGER user on attachment row
steps.visual_check: Attachment row showing download button AND delete button (trash icon) — both action buttons visible for MANAGER role

steps.expected_result: Confirmation dialog appears: 'Are you sure you want to delete this attachment?'
Dialog with confirm/cancel buttons visible
steps.visual_check: Confirmation dialog overlaying the panel with warning text about permanent deletion and Confirm/Cancel buttons

steps.expected_result: Attachment deleted — API call DELETE /attachments/{id} returns 200, list refreshes
Empty state shown: 'No attachments yet' with illustration
steps.visual_check: AttachmentPanel showing empty state with icon and 'No attachments yet' message, count badge removed from header

journeys.name: STAFF User Cannot Delete Attachments
journeys.description: Verify that a STAFF-role user can view and download attachments but the delete button is hidden per RBAC rules.

steps.expected_result: Invoice detail page loads (logged in as STAFF)
AttachmentPanel opens with attachment list
Download button IS visible

steps.target: Delete button absence on attachment row
steps.expected_result: Delete button is NOT visible — hidden for STAFF role (canDelete=false)
steps.visual_check: Attachment row showing ONLY the download button — no trash/delete icon visible, confirming RBAC restriction

journeys.name: Create a General Note
journeys.description: Test creating a GENERAL-type note via the AddNoteForm — verify it appears in the timeline with correct author, timestamp, and grey type badge.

steps.expected_result: Invoice detail page loads

steps.target: Notes tab in the record detail page tabs
steps.expected_result: NotesPanel renders as tab content showing empty state or existing notes
steps.visual_check: Notes tab panel visible with 'Add Note' button/form at the top, timeline area below (empty state if no notes yet)

steps.expected_result: AddNoteForm expands showing textarea and type selector
Type selector shows options: General (default), Internal, Customer Visible — SYSTEM type NOT available

input_data.content: Initial review completed. All line items verified against purchase order PO-2026-0015.

steps.expected_result: Textarea filled, type set to General (default), Add Note button becomes enabled
Note created via POST /notes API, form collapses, timeline refreshes
New note appears at top of timeline with: grey 'General' type badge, current user name, 'just now' timestamp, note content text
steps.visual_check: Notes timeline showing the new note card with: grey badge labelled 'General', author name, relative timestamp 'just now', full note content text, timeline connector line on the left, overflow menu (three dots) for edit/pin actions

journeys.name: Create Internal and Customer-Visible Notes
journeys.description: Test creating notes with INTERNAL and CUSTOMER_VISIBLE types, verify colour-coded type badges match the design spec (Internal=blue, Customer Visible=green).

steps.expected_result: Invoice detail page loads
NotesPanel visible
AddNoteForm expands

input_data.content: Discuss payment terms with finance team before posting. Do not share externally.

steps.expected_result: Form filled with INTERNAL type selected
Internal note created, appears in timeline
Note card shows blue 'Internal' type badge (bg-blue-100 text-blue-700)
steps.visual_check: Timeline with new note showing BLUE badge labelled 'Internal', confirming the note type colour coding

steps.expected_result: AddNoteForm expands again

input_data.content: Your invoice has been received and is being processed. Expected payment within 30 days.

steps.expected_result: Form filled with CUSTOMER_VISIBLE type selected
Customer Visible note created
Note card shows green 'Customer Visible' type badge (bg-green-100 text-green-700)
steps.visual_check: Timeline now showing multiple notes with distinct colour-coded badges: newest 'Customer Visible' (GREEN badge) at top, then 'Internal' (BLUE badge), then 'General' (GREY badge) — all in reverse chronological order

journeys.name: Pin and Unpin Notes
journeys.description: Test pinning a note moves it to the top of the timeline regardless of creation date, and unpinning returns it to chronological position.

steps.expected_result: Invoice detail page loads
NotesPanel shows existing notes in reverse chronological order
Notes ordered: Customer Visible (newest), Internal, General (oldest) — no pins yet

steps.target: Overflow menu (three dots) on the oldest 'General' note
steps.expected_result: Dropdown menu appears with: Edit, Pin, Delete options
Note is pinned — PATCH /notes/{id}/pin called, list reorders
Pinned 'General' note now at TOP of timeline with pin icon, followed by Customer Visible, then Internal
steps.visual_check: Timeline reordered: pinned note at top showing pin icon (lucide Pin) in the header, followed by unpinned notes in reverse chronological order

steps.target: Overflow menu on the pinned 'General' note
steps.expected_result: Menu appears with 'Unpin' option (toggled from Pin)
Note unpinned, returns to chronological position at bottom
Notes back to chronological order: Customer Visible, Internal, General — no pin icons

journeys.name: Edit Own Note and Ownership Restriction
journeys.description: Test that a user can edit their own note inline, but cannot edit notes created by other users (unless MANAGER). Also verify SYSTEM notes are always read-only.

steps.expected_result: Invoice detail page loads as STAFF user
NotesPanel shows notes including ones created by current user

steps.target: Overflow menu on own note (created by current user)
steps.expected_result: Menu shows Edit, Pin options
Note card switches to edit mode — textarea appears with existing content, Save/Cancel buttons visible

input_data.content: Initial review completed. All line items verified. UPDATED: Discrepancy found on line 3.

steps.expected_result: Textarea updated with new content
Note updated via PATCH /notes/{id}, card returns to view mode with updated content
steps.visual_check: Note card back in view mode showing the updated content text, confirming the edit was saved

steps.target: Overflow menu on note created by ANOTHER user
steps.expected_result: Edit option is NOT present in the menu (only Pin visible) — current STAFF user cannot edit other users' notes

journeys.name: System Notes Are Read-Only
journeys.description: Verify that SYSTEM-type notes (generated by the system) are displayed with purple badge, italic styling, and no action buttons — completely read-only regardless of user role.

input_data.precondition: A SYSTEM note exists on this record (e.g., 'Status changed to POSTED by AI')

steps.expected_result: Invoice detail page loads as MANAGER
NotesPanel shows notes including a SYSTEM-type note
System note displays with: purple 'System' badge (bg-purple-100 text-purple-700), italic text styling, NO overflow menu (no edit/delete/pin actions)
steps.visual_check: System note card with PURPLE badge labelled 'System', italic content text, and NO action buttons — completely read-only even for MANAGER role

steps.target: Absence of overflow menu on SYSTEM note
steps.expected_result: No three-dots menu, no edit button, no delete button visible on the SYSTEM note card

journeys.name: Delete Note as MANAGER (Soft Delete)
journeys.description: Test that a MANAGER can delete any note (soft delete), and the note disappears from the timeline. STAFF users should not see delete option.

steps.expected_result: Invoice detail page loads as MANAGER
NotesPanel shows existing notes

steps.target: Overflow menu on any non-SYSTEM note
steps.expected_result: Menu shows Edit, Pin, Delete options (Delete visible because MANAGER)
Confirmation appears before deletion
Note soft-deleted via DELETE /notes/{id} (sets deletedAt), note removed from timeline
steps.visual_check: Timeline updated with the deleted note removed, remaining notes visible in correct order

journeys.name: Create a Manual Record Link
journeys.description: Test the full record link creation flow: open LinksPanel, click Add Link, search for target entity, select link type, submit — verify the new link appears grouped by type.

steps.expected_result: Invoice detail page loads with ActionBar

steps.target: Links button in ActionBar persistent tools zone
steps.expected_result: LinksPanel Sheet opens from the right side
Empty state visible: 'No linked records yet' message with 'Add Link' call-to-action
steps.visual_check: LinksPanel Sheet showing header 'Linked Records', empty state icon and message, 'Add Link' button at top

steps.target: Add Link button (Plus icon) in LinksPanel header
steps.expected_result: AddLinkForm Dialog opens as a modal overlay
Dialog with three inputs: Entity Type selector, Entity Search input, Link Type selector — all initially empty, submit button disabled
steps.visual_check: AddLinkForm modal dialog with: Entity Type dropdown, Entity Search text input (greyed out until type selected), Link Type dropdown, direction preview area, disabled 'Add Link' submit button

steps.expected_result: Dropdown shows available entity types: Customer, Customer Invoice, Sales Order, Purchase Order, etc.

steps.target: Customer option in Entity Type dropdown
steps.expected_result: Entity Type set to 'Customer', Entity Search input becomes active
Typeahead search fires after 300ms debounce, dropdown shows matching entities from GET /entities/search?type=Customer&q=Acme
Dropdown shows matching entities with displayName and optional subtitle
steps.visual_check: Entity search dropdown visible below the input showing matching Customer records with display names (e.g., 'Acme Corporation') and subtitles

steps.expected_result: Entity selected, search input shows selected entity name
Dropdown shows: Created From, Fulfils, Payment For, Credit For, Related To, Parent / Child

steps.target: Related To option in Link Type dropdown
steps.expected_result: Link type set to RELATES_TO, direction preview shows: 'Customer Invoice → Customer'
Direction preview shows 'Customer Invoice → Acme Corporation' with arrow indicator
Record link created via POST /record-links, dialog closes, LinksPanel refreshes
LinksPanel shows 'Related To' group header with count (1), expanded to show the link item: Customer icon, 'Acme Corporation' reference, outgoing arrow (→), link to /crm/customers/{id}
steps.visual_check: LinksPanel showing 'Related To' collapsible group expanded with one link item: entity type icon, display reference, direction arrow (→ outgoing), and the link count badge '(1)' in the panel header

steps.expected_result: LinksPanel closes
Links button in ActionBar now shows count badge '(1)'
steps.visual_check: ActionBar persistent tools showing Links button with badge '(1)' next to the link icon

journeys.name: View Bidirectional Record Links
journeys.description: Test that the LinksPanel displays both outgoing and incoming links with correct direction indicators. A link created from another record pointing TO this one should show as 'incoming'.

input_data.precondition: This invoice has both outgoing links (created FROM this record) and incoming links (other records linking TO this record)

steps.expected_result: Invoice detail page loads
LinksPanel opens showing all bidirectional links
Outgoing link shows right arrow (→) direction indicator with 'outgoing' muted badge
Incoming link shows left arrow (←) direction indicator with 'incoming' muted badge
Links grouped by type with Collapsible sections — each group header shows link type label and count
steps.visual_check: LinksPanel showing multiple collapsible groups (e.g., 'Related To', 'Created From'), each with link items showing direction arrows (→ outgoing, ← incoming), entity icons, and display references

journeys.name: Navigate to Related Record from Link
journeys.description: Test clicking a linked record navigates to that record's detail page via React Router.

steps.expected_result: Invoice detail page loads
LinksPanel opens with existing links

steps.target: Display reference link on a linked Customer record (e.g., 'Acme Corporation')
steps.expected_result: Browser navigates to the Customer detail page at /crm/customers/{customerId}
steps.visual_check: Customer detail page loaded after clicking the link — confirms navigation from invoice record to related customer record works

journeys.name: Delete Links with RBAC (Manual vs System)
journeys.description: Test that STAFF can delete manual links but system-generated links require MANAGER role. Verify the delete button visibility respects these rules.

input_data.precondition: Record has both manual and system-generated links

steps.expected_result: Invoice detail page loads as STAFF
LinksPanel opens showing both manual and system links
Delete button (trash icon) visible on manual link for STAFF user

steps.target: Delete button absence on system-generated link
steps.expected_result: Delete button NOT visible on system-generated link for STAFF user — requires MANAGER
steps.visual_check: LinksPanel showing: manual link WITH delete button visible, system-generated link WITHOUT delete button — demonstrating the RBAC difference

steps.expected_result: Confirmation dialog appears
Manual link deleted via DELETE /record-links/{id}, link removed from panel, count badge updates
Re-navigate as MANAGER
LinksPanel opens
Delete button now visible on system-generated link for MANAGER user
steps.visual_check: System-generated link now showing delete button for MANAGER role — confirms elevated permission allows system link deletion

journeys.name: Reject Duplicate Record Link
journeys.description: Test that creating a duplicate link (same source, target, and type) is rejected with a 409 Conflict error displayed to the user.

input_data.precondition: A RELATES_TO link to Customer already exists

steps.expected_result: Invoice detail page loads
LinksPanel opens showing existing RELATES_TO link
AddLinkForm dialog opens
Form filled with identical source/target/type as existing link
API returns 409 Conflict, error toast displayed to user
Toast shows duplicate link error message (translation key: crossCutting.links.duplicateLink)
steps.visual_check: Error toast notification visible indicating that a link of this type already exists between these records

journeys.name: ActionBar Badge Counts Update Correctly
journeys.description: Test that attachment and link count badges in the ActionBar update in real-time as items are added and removed — badges show count when > 0 and hide when 0.

steps.expected_result: Invoice detail page loads
Attachments button visible with NO count badge (count is 0)
Links button visible with NO count badge (count is 0)
steps.visual_check: ActionBar persistent tools zone showing both Attachments and Links buttons WITHOUT any count badges — both at zero

steps.expected_result: Panel opens
File uploaded successfully
Panel closes
Badge now shows '(1)' on Attachments button
steps.visual_check: ActionBar showing Attachments button WITH badge '(1)' — Links button still without badge

steps.expected_result: LinksPanel opens

steps.target: Add Link button → complete add link flow
steps.expected_result: Link created
Panel closes
Attachments badge '(1)' AND Links badge '(1)' both visible
steps.visual_check: ActionBar persistent tools zone showing BOTH Attachments '(1)' and Links '(1)' count badges next to their respective icons

journeys.name: Mobile Responsive Layout — Full-Screen Panels
journeys.description: Test that on phone breakpoints, AttachmentPanel and LinksPanel render as full-screen overlay sheets instead of side panels, with a visible close button.

steps.expected_result: Invoice detail page loads in mobile viewport
On phone, persistent tools zone is hidden — Attachments and Links available through overflow menu
steps.visual_check: Mobile view of ActionBar showing only primary action button, persistent tools hidden behind overflow menu (hamburger/more)

steps.expected_result: Overflow menu opens showing Attachments and Links options with their counts
AttachmentPanel opens as FULL-SCREEN overlay (not 400px side panel)
Panel takes full viewport width, close button (X) visible at top, upload zone and list fill the screen
steps.visual_check: Full-screen AttachmentPanel overlay covering entire viewport on mobile — close button in top-right, FileUploadZone, and attachment list scrollable below

steps.expected_result: Full-screen panel closes, returning to invoice detail page
LinksPanel opens as FULL-SCREEN overlay
LinksPanel takes full viewport, Add Link button and grouped links visible, close button present
steps.visual_check: Full-screen LinksPanel overlay on mobile — header with 'Linked Records' title and close button, Add Link button, link groups filling the screen

journeys.name: Keyboard Navigation — Panels and ActionBar
journeys.description: Test that all interactive elements in cross-cutting panels are keyboard-navigable: Tab moves through elements, Enter/Space activates buttons, Escape closes panels (WCAG 2.1 AA / NFR27, NFR28).

steps.expected_result: Invoice detail page loads

steps.target: ActionBar Attachments button (keyboard: Tab to button, press Enter)
steps.expected_result: AttachmentPanel opens via keyboard activation
Focus is trapped within the AttachmentPanel Sheet — Tab cycles through: close button, upload zone, attachment list items, action buttons

input_data.keyboard_action: Tab to upload zone, press Enter

steps.expected_result: FileUploadZone has role='button', tabIndex=0, and activates file picker on Enter/Space keypress
AttachmentPanel Sheet closes, focus returns to Attachments button in ActionBar
Arrow keys move focus between ActionBar buttons (primary action, Attachments, Links) following WAI-ARIA toolbar pattern
Attachments button has aria-label 'Attachments (N files)', Links button has aria-label 'Links (N records)' with correct counts

journeys.name: SYSTEM Note Type Not Available in UI
journeys.description: Verify that the AddNoteForm type selector does not include SYSTEM as an option — users cannot create system notes through the UI.

steps.expected_result: Invoice detail page loads
NotesPanel visible
AddNoteForm expands
Dropdown opens showing ONLY: General, Internal, Customer Visible
SYSTEM type is NOT listed in the dropdown — only General, Internal, Customer Visible options available
steps.visual_check: Note type selector dropdown expanded showing exactly three options: 'General' (default/selected), 'Internal', 'Customer Visible' — no 'System' option
